questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: Regarding the composition of the Executive Committee of a National Sports Body, which of the following requirements are mandatory as per the regulatory framework?
contextLines: 1. A minimum of fifteen members in total.
4. Two members specifically from the Athletes Committee.

options.text: All one, two, three, and four

questions.explanation: According to the structure of National Sports Bodies, the Executive Committee must have: at least fifteen members, at least 4 women, at least 2 sportspersons, and 2 members from the athletes committee, along with ex officio and other members.
questions.takeaway: The Executive Committee of a National Sports Body has specific diversity and representation quotas for women and active/former athletes.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Executive Committee: Atleast fifteen members, (atleast 4 women), atleast 2 sportspersons, 2 from athletes committee and ex officio and other members.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: With reference to the institutional reforms under NEP 2020, which of the following pairs is/are correctly matched?
contextLines: 1. PARAKH : National assessment and standard-setting body
2. ABC : Digital storehouse of academic credits for higher education
3. HECI : Regulatory body for school education standards
questions.explanation: Pair 1 is correctly matched: PARAKH is the national assessment body. Pair 2 is correctly matched: ABC (Academic Bank of Credits) is for higher education. Pair 3 is incorrectly matched: HECI (Higher Education Commission of India) is for Higher Education regulatory reform, not school education.
questions.takeaway: NEP 2020 distinguishes between bodies for school assessment (PARAKH) and higher education regulation (HECI).
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Assessment Reforms: PARAKH as national assessment and standard-setting body... Academic Bank of Credits (ABC)... Regulatory Reform: Higher Education Commission of India (HECI) with four verticals.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: Consider the following targets and standards set under the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 for school education:
contextLines: 1. Achieving Foundational Literacy and Numeracy (FLN) for all children by Grade 5.
2. Maintaining a Pupil-Teacher Ratio (PTR) of less than or equal to 30:1.
3. Ensuring a minimum of 50 hours of Continuous Professional Development (CPD) for teachers annually.

options.text: None of the statements are correct

questions.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: The target for Foundational Literacy & Numeracy (FLN) is Grade 3, not Grade 5. Statement 2 is correct: The policy aims for a PTR ≤ 30:1. Statement 3 is correct: Teachers are expected to undergo a minimum of 50 hours of CPD annually.
questions.takeaway: FLN targets are focused on the early primary years (Grade 3), while teacher standards emphasize both ratio and continuous training.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Foundational Literacy & Numeracy (FLN): Reading with comprehension and basic math by Grade 3... Teachers: Pupil-Teacher Ratio ≤ 30:1; minimum 50 hours Continuous Professional Development annually.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes the 'Academic Bank of Credits' (ABC) as envisioned under the National Education Policy 2020?

options.text: A financial repository providing low-interest loans to students in Quality Institutions.
A digital facility to store academic credits to facilitate multiple entry and exit options in higher education.
A centralized database for tracking the 50-hour annual professional development of teachers.
A regulatory vertical under the Higher Education Commission of India for monitoring vocational integration.

questions.explanation: The Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) is designed to support the 3/4-year undergraduate degree structure by allowing students to store credits digitally, enabling them to exit and re-enter the higher education system at different stages.
questions.takeaway: The ABC is a structural tool for flexibility in higher education, distinct from financial or teacher-training platforms.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Quality Institutions: 3/4-year UG degrees with multiple exit options; Academic Bank of Credits (ABC).

questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt:
Consider the following pairs of institutional reforms under NEP 2020 and their primary functions:
1. PARAKH: National assessment and standard-setting body.
2. HECI: Regulatory body for higher education with four verticals.
3. NCIVE: Integration of vocational education.
4. NETF: Technology-driven teacher recruitment and training.

How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Pairs 1, 2, and 3 are correctly matched. PARAKH is the assessment body. HECI is the higher education regulator with four verticals. NCIVE is for vocational integration. Pair 4 is incorrectly matched; while NETF (National Educational Technology Forum) deals with ed-tech integration, teacher training is specifically mentioned as being facilitated via SWAYAM/DIKSHA and the National Mission for Mentoring.
questions.takeaway: NEP 2020 creates specialized bodies for assessment (PARAKH), regulation (HECI), and vocational integration (NCIVE).
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Assessment Reforms: PARAKH as national assessment and standard-setting body. ... Regulatory Reform: Higher Education Commission of India (HECI) with four verticals. ... Other Areas: NCIVE for vocational integration; NETF for ed-tech integ...
